name: Charlie Ventura
abstract: Charlie Ventura (born Charles Venturo; December 2, 1916 – January 17, 1992) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist and bandleader from Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, United States.

concerts.label: Carnegie Pop Concert: Jazz at the Philharmonic

works.t: I Don't Stand a Ghost of a Chance With You

concerts.label: Stan Kenton and His Orchestra: Festival of Modern American Jazz
Midnight Performance: Stan Kenton and His Orchestra: Festival of Modern American Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Shaughnessy Part I
about_quotes.evidence: my first big break... was a band called Charlie Ventura... well, then my first big break with something that really was making a little bit of noise was a band called Charlie Ventura... since this was the first band that I was with that had a real name thing
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Shaughnessy Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 1, 1995 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: I was with Charlie just before joining Woody. I went with Charlie when I was 19... The first record I made was that first trip with Charlie Ventura.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: On that band was Buddy DeFranco on clarinet, and there was Boots Mussulli, and Charlie Ventura was on the band... And I joined the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Candoli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Greg Caputo
about_quotes.evidence: I got to play with these people and learn from them. And Charlie Ventura came through...
about_quotes.doc_title: Greg Caputo interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: John Bunch
about_quotes.evidence: I also worked with Gene Krupa quite a lot in the quartet. With Charlie Ventura.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Bunch interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral
about_quotes.evidence: Charlie is forming a new band, would you two like to join? We said yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998
